title_ar: عمدة القاري شرح صحيح البخاري للعيني — الجزء 3
content_id:
Bagian-bagian ibadah dalam Umdat al-Qari termasuk yang paling penting secara yurisprudensial, menyajikan pembacaan Hanafi atas tradisi shalat, bersuci, puasa, dan haji al-Bukhari dengan ketelitian sistematis. Mazhab Hanafi menganut posisi-posisi tentang metodologi shalat yang berbeda dalam beberapa hal dari posisi Syafi'i yang dipertahankan dalam Fath al-Bari, dan syarah al-Ayni menyediakan artikulasi paling lengkap yang tersedia tentang bagaimana ulama Hanafi memahami bukti hadis kenabian dalam terang posisi-posisi ini.

Bagian-bagian bersuci dalam syarah menyajikan pemahaman Hanafi tentang kebersihan ritual dengan mengacu pada tradisi al-Bukhari tentang topik ini. Posisi-posisi Hanafi tentang zat-zat apa yang merupakan najis, bagaimana wudu dan mandi wajib dilakukan dengan benar, dan syarat-syarat di mana tayammum boleh digunakan semuanya diartikulasikan dengan mengacu pada hadis-hadis yang relevan dan dalam dialog dengan posisi-posisi Syafi'i yang al-Ayni temukan kurang berdasar.

Bagian-bagian shalat sangat kaya. Posisi-posisi Hanafi tentang mengangkat tangan dalam shalat, penempatan tangan selama posisi berdiri, cara melakukan sujud, dan hukum-hukum untuk mengikuti imam semuanya dipertahankan dengan perhatian cermat terhadap bukti hadis. Pembahasan-pembahasan al-Ayni tentang topik-topik ini terlibat langsung dengan pertahanan Syafi'i Ibn Hajar terhadap posisi-posisi yang berbeda, menciptakan dialog yurisprudensial berkelanjutan tentang setiap pertanyaan yang para pelajar dari kedua mazhab temukan instruktif.

Bagian-bagian zakat dan puasa menyajikan posisi-posisi Hanafi tentang sedekah wajib dan puasa dengan ketelitian yang serupa. Fikih Hanafi tentang zakat mencakup beberapa posisi khas tentang kategori kekayaan apa yang wajib dizakati dan pada tarif berapa, dan pembelaan al-Ayni atas posisi-posisi ini dengan mengacu pada tradisi Bukhari merupakan sumber penting bagi ulama yang bekerja pada hukum zakat perbandingan. Bagian-bagian puasa demikian pula membela posisi-posisi Hanafi yang khas tentang syarat-syarat puasa Ramadan dan tindakan-tindakan yang membatalkan atau tidak membatalkan puasa.
content_en:
The worship sections of Umdat al-Qari are among its most juristically important, presenting the Hanafi reading of al-Bukhari's prayer, purification, fasting, and pilgrimage traditions with systematic thoroughness. The Hanafi school held positions on prayer methodology that differed in several respects from the Shafi'i positions defended in Fath al-Bari, and al-Ayni's commentary provides the fullest available articulation of how Hanafi scholars understood the prophetic hadith evidence in light of these positions.

The purification sections of the commentary present the Hanafi understanding of ritual cleanliness with reference to the al-Bukhari traditions on this topic. Hanafi positions on what substances constitute impurity, how ablution and the full bath are properly performed, and the conditions under which the dry ablution (tayammum) may be used are all articulated with reference to the relevant hadiths and in dialogue with the Shafi'i positions that al-Ayni finds insufficiently supported.

The prayer sections are particularly rich. The Hanafi positions on the raising of hands during prayer, the placement of the hands during the standing position, the manner of performing the prostrations, and the rulings for following an imam are all defended with careful attention to the hadith evidence. Al-Ayni's discussions of these topics engage directly with Ibn Hajar's Shafi'i defenses of different positions, creating a sustained juristic dialogue on each question that students of both schools find instructive.

The zakah and fasting sections present the Hanafi positions on almsgiving and fasting with similar thoroughness. Hanafi fiqh on zakah includes some distinctive positions on what categories of wealth are subject to zakah and at what rates, and al-Ayni's defenses of these positions with reference to the Bukhari traditions are important resources for scholars working on comparative zakah law. The fasting sections similarly defend the specifically Hanafi positions on the conditions of the Ramadan fast and the acts that break or do not break it.
content_ar: The worship sections of Umdat al-Qari are among its most juristically المهم, presenting the الحنفي reading of البخاري's الصلاة, purification, الصيام, and pilgrimage traditions with systematic thoroughness. The الحنفي school held positions on الصلاة methodology that differed in several respects from the الشافعي positions defended in Fath al-Bari, and al-Ayni's commentary provides the fullest available articulation of how الحنفي العلماء understood the prophetic الحديث evidence in light of these positions. The purification sections of the commentary present the الحنفي الفهم of ritual cleanliness with reference to the البخاري traditions on this topic. الحنفي positions on what substances constitute impurity, how ablution and the full bath are properly performed, and the conditions under which the dry ablution (التيمم) may be used are all articulated with reference to the relevant hadiths and in dialogue with the الشافعي positions that al-Ayni finds insufficiently supported. The الصلاة sections are particularly rich. The الحنفي positions on the raising of hands خلال الصلاة, the placement of the hands خلال the standing position, the manner of performing the prostrations, and the rulings for following an الإمام are all defended with careful attention to the الحديث evidence. Al-Ayni's discussions of these topics engage directly with Ibn Hajar's الشافعي defenses of different positions, creating a sustained juristic dialogue on each question that students of both schools find instructive. The الزكاة and الصيام sections present the الحنفي positions on almsgiving and الصيام with similar thoroughness. الحنفي الفقه on الزكاة includes some distinctive positions on what categories of الثروة are subject to الزكاة and at what rates, and al-Ayni's defenses of these positions with reference to the البخاري traditions are المهم resources for العلماء working on comparative الزكاة law. The الصيام sections similarly defend the specifically الحنفي positions on the conditions of the Ramadan fast and the acts that break or do not break it.
